steps.title: Use the y-intercept
steps.content: A $y$-intercept has $x=0$. Since the intercept is $(k-5,b)$, $$k-5=0$$ $$k=5$$

steps.title: Find the slope from the table
steps.content: The table gives the two points $(k,13)$ and $(k+7,-15)$. Their slope is $$m=(-15-13)/((k+7)-k)=-28/7=-4.$$

steps.title: Move from the table point to the intercept
steps.content: From $(k,13)$ to the intercept $(k-5,b)$, the $x$-value decreases by $5$. With slope $-4$, moving left $5$ makes $y$ increase by $20$: $$b=13+20=33.$$ The answer is 33.
